title: Writing - Part 1: Homelessness
description: Your class has been discussing the causes of homelessness and possible solutions to this social problem.

content.question: Write an essay discussing two of the causes in your notes. You should explain which cause you consider most significant, giving reasons for your opinion.

opinions: Rising property prices mean that low-income families and individuals cannot afford to rent or buy a home.
Many homeless people struggle with untreated mental illness or substance abuse, making it hard to maintain stable housing.
Without a regular income, people quickly fall behind on rent and risk losing their homes.

structure.introduction: Introduce homelessness as a serious social issue affecting many countries.
structure.bodyParagraph1: Discuss the first cause and its role in driving homelessness.
structure.bodyParagraph2: Discuss the second cause and argue which is more fundamental.
structure.conclusion: Summarise your analysis and suggest how society could best address the root causes.

content.modelAnswer:
Homelessness is a complex social problem that affects thousands of people in even the wealthiest nations. Understanding its root causes is essential for developing effective solutions. Two of the most significant factors are the lack of affordable housing and unemployment.

In many cities, property prices and rents have risen far faster than wages, leaving low-income workers unable to afford stable accommodation. When a single unexpected crisis such as illness or redundancy occurs, these individuals quickly find themselves without a home. The shortage of social housing has made this problem considerably worse in recent decades.

Unemployment is equally central to the issue. Without a reliable income, it is almost impossible to maintain housing or escape a cycle of poverty. Many homeless people lack the qualifications or support needed to find work, creating a situation that is very difficult to break out of independently.

In conclusion, while both causes are deeply interlinked, I believe addressing the shortage of affordable housing is the most urgent priority, as it would provide a foundation from which other problems can be tackled.
